programme.title: Finding Bigfoot: Untold Stories
programme.desc: Follow the bigfoot team in their natural habitat for a glimpse into their lives when they're not squatching.

programme.title: I Was Prey
programme.desc: A chimpanzee at an animal sanctuary escapes its enclosure and chases down a volunteer. Then, a surfer is ready to catch a wave when a great white shark knocks him off his board and attacks him.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Donut Mania is Brett's newest business venture and they're having a grand opening. Wayde and the team want to congratulate him with a surprise fish tank!

programme.title: Rugged Justice
programme.desc: Officer Olson catches repeat offenders poaching more than five times the legal limit of squid. Officer Summit serves a warrant to a bear hunter breaking the law.

programme.title: Wolves And Warriors
programme.desc: Matt is accompanied by Lorin and his team from the Lockwood Animal Rescue Center as they head to Oregon to rescue a grey wolf named Sadira; US Army veteran Albert hopes to reconnect with his family by joining the rescue team.

programme.title: Saving Britain's Wildlife
programme.desc: A fox with an injured leg must be caught before it can be treated. A tawny owl stuck down a sooty chimney needs specialist care to restore its eyesight.

programme.title: Dr. Dee: Alaska Vet
programme.desc: Dr. Dee investigates a possible neurological problem in her own dog. A horse castration turns dangerous, and a potential case of animal neglect is not what it seems.

programme.title: Little Giants
programme.desc: The guys are in Mpumalanga, South Africa, searching for the Cape mole-rat. They have giant, razor-sharp teeth, and are known as the 'king of the underground'.

programme.title: Clinically Wild: Alaska
programme.desc: A snowboarding pit bull sustains injuries on the slopes; a husky requires emergency surgery.

programme.title: Clinically Wild: Alaska
programme.desc: Dr. Kesting treats a wounded cat that had been ``home-treated'' with duct tape; Dr. Doty examines a stubborn great horned owl.

programme.title: The Last Alaskans
programme.desc: The race begins to secure food and shelter before the snow arrives. Heimo's family hunts for caribou, but will a hungry bear stop them in their tracks?

programme.title: Rugged Justice
programme.desc: The Washington State Fish and Wildlife Police confront a lying hunter. Later, they have to deal with anglers illegally snagging salmon.

programme.title: The Zoo
programme.desc: Jasmine, a 14-year-old otter, is not her usual, energetic self. And, the staff of the New York Aquarium move their sharks to a new exhibit.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees
programme.desc: A disabled veteran and his wife come to Villalobos to find a pair of dogs to help with his depression. Marcel and Mariah race to help a puppy in need.

programme.title: Black Mako Of The Abyss
programme.desc: Marine biologist Craig O'Connell leads a team to investigate a shark, which displays traits of both great whites and makos. They uncover astonishing evidence that challenges existing knowledge about sharks.

programme.title: Extinct Or Alive
programme.sub-title: Ivory Billed Woodpecker Of The Bayou
programme.desc: Forrest is determined to find out if the famously reclusive Ivory Billed Woodpecker still exists in the alligator and snake infested swamps of Louisiana.

programme.desc: Sci-fi romance starring Alex Sharp and Elle Fanning. It's 1977 and, in Croydon, a teenage punk schoolboy encounters a beautiful young woman from outer space. Strong language.

programme.title: Nowhere Boy

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: Grissom, Nick, and Warrick take the case of a jogger who was killed by a wild animal in the park. It gets complicated when they discover that the jogger had his liver removed after he was killed.

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: A woman is abducted from a mall parking garage. She's raped, beaten, shot and left for dead. Sara collects evidence from her and grows attached to her.

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: Grissom and Catherine's robbery/homicide investigation leads to a bizarre discovery, while Warrick and Sara try to solve the case of a woman burnt to ashes.

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: When Grissom, Warrick, Sara and Nick visit the site of a fatal carjacking, they must deal with the fact that heavy rainfall has washed away much of the crucial evidence.

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: The team is called upon to examine the evidence in the case of an infant kidnapping.

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: The CSI team investigate a fatal vehicular accident involving a local deaf man, and Catherine and Nick become involved in a multiple homicide case.

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: On the day Grissom has to evaluate his team, a head is found in the trunk of a car. While Gil and Catherine work on the head, Sara and Nick investigate a body in the desert that's missing it's head.

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: The team is investigating the ongoing murders by a signature killer. The killer has some knowledge of forensics and has left little evidence behind.

programme.desc: When the son of Las Vegas mogul is found dead, the CSI team come together to investigate the cause of death.

programme.desc: The CSI team is called to investigate the case of a missing college freshman whose bags are packed but who is nowhere to be found.

programme.desc: Grissom investigates a possible suicide at a high-profile construction site, while Catherine and Nick look into the case of a young boy found dead at his therapy session,

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation

programme.desc: Grissom, Sara and Warrick are called on to investigate an abandoned, blood-splattered apartment and Nick and Catherine must examine a dead scuba diver lodged in a tree.

programme.desc: Grissom and his team investigate a murder suspect who is caught red-handed burying the victims. Meanwhile, Catherine and Warrick take on the case of a woman found dead in a hotel spa.

programme.desc: Take an in-depth look at the life and crimes of William Devin Howell who was found guilty of the murders of seven different women.

programme.title: Scroll. Click. Kill.
programme.desc: Puts a spotlight on murder cases that were solved either by online sleuths or the incriminating evidence left behind on the victim or perpetrator's social media profiles.

programme.title: Australian Crime Stories
programme.desc: In 1969, Lucille Butterworth disappears from a bus stop in Tasmania. Seven years later, Geoffrey Charles Hunt confesses to the crime but despite damning evidence, police did not lay charges.

programme.title: Australian Crime Stories
programme.desc: Famous designer Florence Broadhurst gets bludgeoned to death in her own factory in 1977. An exploration of whether she was a victim of an enemy or of a serial killer.

programme.title: The Social Media Murders
programme.desc: Days after 27-year-old Jade Ward ended her relationship with her controlling husband Russell Marsh, she was murdered. Digital forensics, unseen body cam and police interviews are used to get justice for Jade.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Take an in-depth look at the life and crimes of William Devin Howell who was found guilty of the murders of seven different women.

programme.title: Scroll. Click. Kill.
programme.desc: Puts a spotlight on murder cases that were solved either by online sleuths or the incriminating evidence left behind on the victim or perpetrator's social media profiles.

programme.title: The First 48
programme.desc: Mobile, Alabama Detective Mark Kennedy is called to a scene where a young father has been shot to death, and the initial clues point toward a conflict that may have turned fatal.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter
programme.desc: When uninvited guests crash a birthday party, violence erupts, leaving Enrique Urrutia dead and potentially sparking a gang war. To keep the peace, Lt Joe Kenda must crack an unwritten code of silence.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter
programme.desc: When 13-year-old PJ Grant is found dead from a single gunshot wound to the head, Lt Kenda assumes it's a suicide. When the physical evidence doesn't add up, it opens the door to a world of possibilities.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: A man steals his girlfriend's truck when she refuses to take him to get methadone. A customer in Key West argues with his Russian girlfriend. A woman addicted to fentanyl steals her parent's truck.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: An impulsive teenager can't stomach the magnitude of his mistake; one woman learns a lesson in not accepting rides from strangers; marital issues spiral out of control when a husband picks his wife up from jail.

programme.title: Australian Crime Stories: The Investi...
programme.desc: No body, no witnesses, no DNA. Detective Dennis Bray reveals how he built the case that convicted killer Bruce Burrell for the murders of Dorothy Davis and Kerry Whelan.

programme.title: Making A Serial Killer
programme.desc: Hurricane Katrina claimed a lot of lives - but some of the bodies of men which turned up in southern Louisiana were actually killed by a short, fat man nobody suspected of being a serial killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives
programme.desc: DNA and genealogy reveal serial killer Jerry McFadden murdered Anna Hlavka in 1979 in Portland. Featuring witness testimony, new interviews, archive footage and dramatic reconstructions.

programme.title: Targeted For Murder
programme.desc: Barbara Weaver, an Amish woman, is murdered. Investigators uncover a hidden killer within the Amish community, challenging the notion of peace-loving Amish people.

programme.desc: Does democratised culture mean better art or is true talent instead drowned out? A documentary film containing interviews with some of the world's most influential creators of the digital era.

programme.desc: The full-length feature directorial debut of artist and photographer Steven Sebring, Patti Smith: Dream of Life offers an intimate glimpse into the philosophy and artistry of legendary singer, artist and poet Patti Smith.

programme.title: Shipping The World
programme.desc: Decades of shifting shipping routes have been shaped by one goal: speed & safety. But treacherous oceans & pirate waters off Somalia still threaten vessels to this day.

programme.title: Shipping The World

programme.title: The Faulkner Focus
programme.desc: Host Harris Faulkner presents the latest political news stories, provides insightful commentary on current events, and interviews top newsmakers.

programme.title: Outnumbered

programme.title: The Story
programme.desc: Martha MacCallum brings the story of the times with her tough but fair interviews and straightforward analysis; "The Story" captures the voices that need to be heard and the people at the center of every history-shaping moment.

programme.title: The Will Cain Show
programme.desc: The Will Cain Show features Cains signature podcast style and thoughtful perspective featuring conversations with newsmakers across politics, culture and sports.

programme.title: The Five
programme.desc: A five-member panel of Fox News Channel personalities talks about the most notable news items of the day in a roundtable format, including current events, political stories from the United States and elsewhere, and pop culture stories.

programme.desc: Hannity, hosted by Sean Hannity, showcases his candid, provocative style and conservative commentary on politics and the American agenda.

programme.title: The Patriot
programme.desc: Peaceful farmer Benjamin Martin is driven to lead the Colonial Militia during the American Revolution when a sadistic British officer murders his son.

programme.desc: When a killer shark unleashes chaos on a beach community off Cape Cod, it's up to a local sheriff, a marine biologist, and an old seafarer to hunt the beast down.

programme.desc: The wackiest head counsellor in North Star camp history, Tripper Harrison devotes his summer activities to cracking jokes, chasing female staffers, and plotting the demise of their rivals.

programme.title: Coming To America
programme.desc: In an unforgettable comic quest to the New World, it's time for an African Prince to find a princess. The mission leads him and his most loyal friend to New York.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George panics when there is a false fire alarm at a kid's birthday party, abandoning the women and children in his attempted escape; Kramer starts dating a woman, who works with Elaine; Jerry gets revenge on a heckler.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: The gang makes a special trip to the Hamptons for a weekend to see a friend's new baby; George becomes upset when he discovers that his girlfriend enjoys topless sunbathing; Kramer steals lobsters and gets into trouble with the law.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George's life turns completely around when he adopts a new opposite philosophy; Kramer appears on "Regis and Kathy Lee" to promote his new coffee table book; Jerry agrees when Rachael tells him that she wants to break up.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Kramer chaperones Jerry's date with a Miss America hopeful.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George gets upset when his girlfriend is thanked for a dinner he bought; Jerry becomes obsessed when he finds that his current girlfriend once dated Newman; Elaine goes to a stationary store, where the clerk flirts with her.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Kramer finds Jerry's old birthday checks from his grandmother and insists he deposits them, which puts Jerry's nana in unexpected debt; Elaine mistakes a high-talking man for a woman on the phone.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry feels misled when his mysterious Chinese date ends up not being Chinese; Elaine ruins her close friend's latest relationship with a man who likes to talk; George becomes miserable when his parents decide to separate.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry's new couch is completely ruined when Elaine upsets a man with a bladder condition; Kramer decides to start up his own new pizza business; George joins a book club, but instead of reading the books, he rents the movies.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry's excitement about dating a Romanian Olympic gymnast suddenly diminishes after a bad intimate experience; George's girlfriend is shocked when she catches him eating out of the trash; Kramer passes a kidney stone.

programme.desc: Jerry accepts an Armani suit from Kenny Bania, provided that he will treat him to a meal at Mandy's; Elaine dates a bouncer from England, who turns out to have a few secrets; Kramer decides to start a new diet of only fresh foods.

programme.desc: George decides to buy a convertible that a car salesman claims was once owned by Jon Voight; Kramer attempts to save a small shoe-repair business; Elaine wins Mr. Pitt tickets to the renowned Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade.

programme.desc: A grade school acquaintance puts pressure on Jerry to finally settle an old score in the form of a race; Elaine is blacklisted from a Chinese restaurant; George responses to a personal advertisement in a communist newspaper.

programme.desc: Jerry attempts an impossible switch when he finds that he likes his girlfriend's roommate more than her; George is convinced that his new girlfriend is bulimic; Kramer decides to be himself and starts going by his first name.

programme.desc: Jerry and Elaine experience "re-gifting" and "de-gifting", George loves his girlfriend's apartment, while Kramer and Newman become engrossed in a game of Risk.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George starts to move on with his life after Susan's death. Susan's parents start a foundation in Susan's memory and want George to run it.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George begins to think that the foundation suspects that George was the one that killed Susan. To find out for sure, George hides a tape recorder in a brief case and leaves it in a foundation meeting.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George uses Susan's death to pick up hot women. Jerry's latest girlfriend has 'man hands', while Elaine's new beau is Jerry's exact opposite. Kramer stumbles into a white-collar job at a big firm.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Elaine's strange dancing loses her employees' respect. George cultivates a 'bad boy' image, and Jerry becomes an underground legend after he makes a bootleg video tape of a new movie.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George tries to woo a photo clerk with pictures of himself. Elaine's reputation blocks treatment. Kramer offers to secure a refund for Jerry's busted stereo.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry's new girlfriend has a friend who is being mentored by Kenny Bania, and later, Jerry becomes Kenny's mentor. Meanwhile, George learns about risk management by listening to tapes, and Kramer has Frank help him cook a Jewish meal.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry receives his royalty checks for his appearance on a Japanese TV show and gets writer's cramp after signing them all. Kramer helps some Japanese tourists by finding them sleeping quarters in a chest of drawers.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: A Kenny Rogers Roasters restaurant opens in the area. Elaine gets audited by Peterman for excessive spending. George tries to retrieve a Russian hat.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Abstaining from sex has opposite effects on Elaine and George. Jerry gets bumped from career day at his high school. Kramer opens a smoking lounge.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George squares off against a shipwreck survivor over a new apartment; Jerry attempts to help Newman get a much sought-after transfer to Hawaii.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry bounces a check at a market and the owner puts it up for everyone to see. Jerry soon finds that the owner runs an underground cock fight, so Jerry and Kramer train Kramer's rooster, named Little Jerry, to beat the big rooster.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry buys back a car his parents sold as the Costanzas decide to spend George's inheritance; Kramer can't sleep with his new girlfriend, while Peterman returns unexpectedly.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: As Jerry discovers he's been duped by a hapless tennis pro, George seeks revenge against a co-worker's cutting remarks; Kramer enlists Elaine's help in drafting a living will.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Kramer gets mugged by a gang, and upon hearing the story, Peterman wants Kramer's life experiences for his own autobiography; everyone seems to object to Jerry's new girlfriend, who he sees as perfect.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Elaine decides to have an alter-ego when a co-worker mistakenly calls her Susie. Susie gets blamed for everything at work and Elaine becomes more popular because she is the only one that ever can find Susie.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry accidentally drops his girlfriend's toothbrush in the toilet and she uses it before he can tell her about it. Not wanting to tell her the truth, Jerry tries to sterilize her mouth and refuses to kiss her.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Elaine keeps getting dragged to see the The English Patient even though she absolutely hates the film. A gorgeous woman mistakes George for her boyfriend, Neil. Jerry takes on The Mandelbaum Boys.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George builds a bed under his desk. Elaine gets a mattress from her back conscious boyfriend. Jerry wrangles an indecisive carpenter, and Kramer takes extreme measures to swim.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George's new flame keeps including "yada yada" in her stories. Jerry is offended by Whatley's Jewish jokes. Elaine tries to help Beth and her new husband adopt a baby. Kramer and Mickey double date.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Kramer prepares for his millennium party, Jerry jockeys for position on his girlfriend's speed dial, Elaine gets irked by an inattentive saleswoman, and George tries to get sacked by The Yankees.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: A muffin shop starts selling the tops of muffins, but runs into problems when the muffin bottoms begin to pile up. Kramer tries to get rid of the bottoms for the shop, but is turned down by everyone he offers them to.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Kramer's job at the Tony Awards leads to a meeting with Raquel Welch; George helps Jerry date a high-maintenance woman; Elaine is labeled catty after making a comment about a co-worker.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George lands a job after being mistaken as handicapped. Jerry suspects Bania is using him. Kramer's shaving trick backfires, and Elaine ends a relationship mid flight.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: As Jerry risks losing a girlfriend over a joke, George continues to work despite having been asked to leave; Elaine finds breaking up is hard to do as Kramer hires an assistant.

programme.desc: Jerry's new girlfriend has a friend who is being mentored by Kenny Bania, and later, Jerry becomes Kenny's mentor. Meanwhile, George learns about risk management by listening to tapes, and Kramer has Frank help him cook a Jewish meal.

programme.title: According To Jim
programme.desc: Jim is feeling rather depressed about the recent passing of a good friend, but all of his sympathy flies out the window when he discovers that the former running mate is to be buried with a prized baseball card that he once stole from Jim.

programme.title: Scrubs
programme.desc: My Inconvenient Truth: JD has to face his own inconvenient truth when his brother Dan returns to town and tells him that it's time to grow up. (S7 Ep3/11)

programme.title: Scrubs
programme.desc: Turk tries to assure a worried Carla that their daughter will acknowledge her Latin heritage. The Janitor challenges J.D. to identify everyone in the hospital.

programme.title: Scrubs
programme.desc: My Growing Pains: The Sacred Heart staff have to deal with their inner child when JD and Turk remember their first prank and Dr Kelso celebrates his birthday. (S7 Ep5/11)

programme.title: The Nanny
programme.desc: While still shocked about finding out about Niles and CC's secret affair, Fran and Maxwell worry about something else. Max's biggest chance to beat Andrew Lloyd Webber at the Tonys is going to be wasted.

programme.title: The Nanny
programme.desc: Michael got a modelling job in Japan, so he proposes to Maggie so they don't have to be apart. Maxwell has a hard time accepting the fact that his little daughter has grown up.

programme.title: The Nanny
programme.desc: After a fortune-teller predicts that Maxwell will end up in bed with a blue-eyed blonde while he and C.C. are on a business trip, Fran and Niles decide to follow their respective partners to ensure that they do not get together.

programme.title: Mad About You
programme.desc: Upon learning of his parent's plans to drive to Buffalo, Paul worries that his father's old car won't survive the trip. But when Burt refuses to listen, Jamie suggests that she and Paul steal the car.

programme.title: Mad About You
programme.desc: After hearing about the unexpected death of their parcel deliveryman, Jamie and Paul decide to make an effort to tell everybody who's important to them that they love them.

programme.title: Alf
programme.desc: Since Melmacians never get sick, ALF is humiliated when a human cold virus causes the alien to become seriously ill.This episode was produced in season III but aired in season IV.

programme.title: Alf
programme.desc: When Willie and Kate decide to throw a big party for their friends, there's only one problem: What are they going to do with ALF?

programme.title: The Office
programme.desc: Michael takes the Dunder Mifflin crew on a freezing motivational booze cruise in January in an attempt to get them to accept his theories on leadership. To his frustration, they find the ship's captain more inspiring.

programme.title: The Office
programme.desc: Michael demands attention from the entire Dunder Mifflin office after he has a freak accident with his George Foreman grill, meanwhile Dwight begins behaving strangely following a minor car crash.

programme.desc: Desperate to keep Michael from spilling the beans about Pam, Jim tolerates a trip to Hooters.

programme.title: Land Of Primates
programme.desc: As humans take up more space, the baboons of Africa must adapt. Travel across the continent to watch them form unbreakable social bonds, serve to protect their families and survive a changing landscape.

programme.title: Seasons Of The Wild
programme.desc: In Southern Florida, a unique and complex ecosystem known as the Everglades has entered its winter season. Predator and prey are forced to live in close proximity due to a limited supply of water.

programme.title: Seasons Of The Wild
programme.desc: Waters flood Southern Florida's mangroves and marshes. Many of the 600 animal species that live in the Everglades disperse during this time, expanding their hunting and breeding grounds.

programme.title: Wildlife ER
programme.desc: Spoonbill Soft Shoe Shuffle: The team help a trio of troubled baby wallabies and a koala having seizures. Plus, a kookaburra has surgery and a spoonbill gets corrective shoes.

programme.title: Wildlife ER
programme.desc: The clinic deals with the aftermath of a Brisbane flood, while a baby kangaroo is in need of round-the-clock care. Vet Karina tackles the turtle waiting list.

programme.title: Wildlife ER
programme.desc: Vet Karina fights to save a koala mauled by dogs. Plus, rescue Chantel tries to pick up a peeved possum, while vet Meaghan bonds with a gaggle of goslings.

programme.title: CROCS OF KATUMA
programme.desc: Persevere with a mother crocodile as she struggles to survive and raise her young in one of Africa's most seasonally volatile and unforgiving rivers, the Katuma. Endure hunger and danger as she guards her clutch with ruthless precision.

programme.title: Arabian Inferno
programme.desc: It sometimes feels like mammals rule the world; there are well over 5,000 species, colonising every corner of the globe. But in Arabia there's a different story - this is a land where reptiles rule.

programme.title: Arctic Secrets
programme.desc: On the north-east coast of Baffin Island, in Canada's Arctic, lies Ninginganiq, a blustery wilderness where only the intrepid travel. In fall, it is so dangerous that even seasoned Inuit sailors keep away.

programme.title: Cheetah Against All Odds
programme.desc: Follow sisters Koppie and Nyika as they fight to ensure their cubs' survival in the Serengeti. Watch these cheetah mothers face tests in their differing territories, evade ravenous predators, and teach their babies how to endure life in ...

programme.title: Ocean Parks
programme.desc: Overfishing and tourism have landed much of this formerly pristine reef in jeopardy, putting its sharks and turtles in grave danger.

programme.title: Ocean Parks
programme.desc: The conservation efforts of scientists, tour guides and law enforcement in the Exuma Cays are detailed to reveal how the reefs and coral have been actively protected ever since businessman Howard Hughes tried to purchase the islands.

programme.title: Tales From Zambia
programme.desc: Follow a herd of elephants from sunrise to sunset. In a valley full of life, they encounter many colourful characters. But it all changes when the drought comes.

programme.title: Tales From Zambia
programme.desc: In Zambia's Luangwa Valley some of the largest animals on Earth live side by side. Discover how each of these giants uses ingenious strategies and amazing adaptations, honed over millions of years, to survive-and thrive.

programme.title: Tales From Zambia
programme.sub-title: Life And Death On The Plains
programme.desc: Liuwa Plains is a remote grassland and one of Africa's last wild places. Only the toughest of Africa's wildlife can survive here. Witness an extraordinary story of perseverance, adaptation, and survival in this extreme habitat.

programme.desc: Lady Elliot Island is the crown jewel of the Great Barrier Reef- the largest living structure on Earth. This southern-most island of the Reef chain is home to dizzying swirls of colour, life and diversity both above and below the water.

programme.title: Arabian Inferno
programme.sub-title: Life and Death in the Dunes
programme.desc: The Arabian Desert is one of the most challenging places on Earth. While the animals may be resilient, they know that death is never too far away.

programme.title: Coastal Africa
programme.desc: Beneath the warm waters of the Indian Ocean lies a sanctuary filled with dazzling aquatic life. From witnessing a nesting turtle's journey to meeting fish-cleaning shrimp, embark on a grand adventure into this amazing marine wilderness.

programme.title: The Big Dry
programme.desc: The Luangwa Valley in Zambia experiences one of the toughest dry seasons imaginable. Seven months with no rain and spiraling temperatures. And with this year's El Nino conditions the drought is threatening to be even more intense than us...

programme.title: The Big Dry
programme.desc: In Zambia's Luangwa Valley, the global El Nino event makes the dry season hotter and harsher than ever.

programme.title: The Big Dry
programme.desc: There's been no rain for half a year. Lagoons have dried to hard, cracked mud. The scars of waterless streams and creeks crisscross the land.

programme.title: African Bambi
programme.desc: Set in eastern Africa, "African Bambi" follows a diverse collection of gazelles on a journey of growth and survival that will test their limits, and captures the unique beauty of one of the most spectacular places on earth.

programme.title: Arabian Inferno
programme.desc: Wadis are small waterholes in the desert and are vital resources for the creatures that live there. However, danger lurks as predators join the party.

programme.title: Attack And Defend

programme.title: Wild Survivors

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: FRONTRUNNERS
programme.desc: Yabusame is traditional horseback archery. It's preserved as a Shinto ritual, but is not accessible to all. Kamimura Ayuko runs competitions open to women and international participants.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo
programme.desc: Pineapples are grown on Japan's southern islands, the northernmost limit of their cultivation. New varieties continue to emerge, and pineapples left to fully ripen in the field are charming consumers.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Dive in Tokyo
programme.sub-title: Akabane - Thriving Between the River and the Plateau
programme.desc: Akabane is a hub in northern Tokyo known for its drinking scene. We discover its roots as a riverside lodging town and explore a plateau where a housing complex now sits on former military grounds.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: The Garden Way
programme.desc: Deeply rooted in Zen culture, karesansui, or dry landscape gardens, use rocks and gravel to convey diverse images to the mind's eye. We explore Kyoto's Myoshin-ji and the secrets of its Zen gardens.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Direct Talk #274
programme.desc: Kanbayashi Takashi has developed a smart pig farming system that leverages the latest AI technology to improve production efficiency. He talks about how it works and his vision for the future.

programme.title: JAPAN'S TOP INVENTIONS
programme.desc: Discover the stories behind renowned Japanese products alongside our reporter. This time: cosmetic sponges.

programme.title: NEWSROOM TOKYO
programme.desc: This program gives you comprehensive coverage of events in Japan and Asia. It explains the day's developments and what is behind them.

programme.title: Direct Talk #258
programme.sub-title: Establishing a Pediatric Hub in Asia
programme.desc: Yoshioka Hideto has led the international NGO Japan Heart for over 20 years, providing free medical support in developing nations. He shares plans to establish a hub for pediatric care in Cambodia.

programme.title: Herbal Symphony
programme.desc: In Uda, discover Japan's oldest private medicinal herb garden, where the natural landscape from 300 years ago is preserved in a botanical time capsule, a sanctuary of the local culture and history.

programme.title: Ukiyoe EDO-LIFE
programme.sub-title: A Napping Boy Dreams of Monsters
programme.desc: When a little boy's nap is disturbed by a bad dream, his mother comes to his rescue. What was terrorizing him? We examine a print that shows how much the Edo townspeople cherished their kids.

programme.title: Science View
programme.sub-title: Esports: The Science Behind the Thrill
programme.desc: Esports has evolved into a global phenomenon. Explore the science behind the excitement, from top players' skills to its potential for healthy aging.

programme.desc: Yabusame is traditional horseback archery. It's preserved as a Shinto ritual, but is not accessible to all. Kamimura Ayuko runs competitions open to women and international participants.

programme.title: Ukiyoe EDO-LIFE
programme.desc: On those hot, humid summer nights, nothing can get in the way of sleep like mosquitoes on the hunt. We learn about the tools the Edo townspeople had at their disposal to fight the pesky insects.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Design X Stories
programme.desc: Today's theme: A New Look at Shadows. Japanese have long found beauty in shadows, but new discoveries still astonish us in art and design. Discover how these designs can change how we see the world.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Rockie and Her ...
programme.desc: Puppet action show for children. In a distant post-human future where surviving creatures have formed a community, new-breed dinosaur girl Rockie sets out on hilarious adventures with her schoolmates.

programme.title: TAKE TECH
programme.desc: A program for upper primary to lower secondary school children to explore the everyday technologies all around them. Each episode takes a piece of familiar tech to find out what it does and how.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.sub-title: Putting Himself Into Every Grain: Seki Tomoharu, Rice Farmer
programme.desc: Seki Tomoharu once turned his back on farming; now he grows legendary rice that wins international awards. Why did he change? Amid rice shortages and global warming, he pursues his own green farming.

programme.title: RAMEN JAPAN 5min.
programme.desc: A gourmet tour of ramen all around Japan. This time: the birthplace of tonkotsu, Fukuoka. Looking at three different styles, we'll learn this ramen's secrets, and the story of its creation.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: BOSAI:Science that Can Save Your Life
programme.desc: The hazard map for Mt. Fuji was revised in 2021, revealing the possibility of greater damage from lava flows. We'll look at what lies behind those assumptions and what countermeasures can be taken.

programme.title: DESIGN MUSEUM JAPAN
programme.desc: Sato Taku (Graphic designer) visits Miyazaki. The crowded signs of local bars inspire Sato to reconsider the conventions of modern design, and embrace the appeal of the unknown.

programme.title: KABUKI KOOL
programme.sub-title: The Revenge of the Soga Brothers
programme.desc: The popular, but true story of two brothers who avenged their father inspired a dazzling variety of so many kabuki plays and dances. that they formed a new genre. Actor Ainosuke explores further.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: NEO-JAPONISM
programme.desc: The Bon Odori dance, a Japanese summer tradition, has gone global. We look at events held in Malaysia and Brazil and explore the rhythms that bring people from diverse backgrounds together.

programme.title: A Culture Built with Wood
programme.desc: Kishu, in the southern part of the Kii Peninsula, is famous among chefs worldwide for its charcoal production. Join us as we examine what fuels the eco-friendly charcoal culture of Kishu.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo
programme.desc: The word yakumi refers to the use of traditional herbs and spices. Japanese food wouldn't be the same without favorites like wasabi or yuzu. See how these and more are influencing global food trends.

programme.title: Darwin's Amazing Animals
programme.sub-title: Conservation through Collaboration - White-Bellied Heron, Bhutan

programme.desc: One of the most appealing features of Japan's national road network is the various Michinoeki, literally way stations, that provide a place for motorists to rest, dine and shop.

programme.desc: Japan's plastic models are popular worldwide. Shizuoka City leads the nation in plastic model shipments. We look at the city's plastic model industry, from its history to the latest developments.

programme.title: I Love 'Mee'
programme.sub-title: Why Is My Birth a Wonderful Thing?
programme.desc: Today is Mee's birthday. Everyone offers birthday wishes, but Mee wonders, "Why do we say 'Happy Birthday'?" So Papa decides to take Mee on a drive to retrace "The Story of Mee's Birth."

programme.title: Design Ah neo
programme.desc: Design is fun! Design feels good! Cutting-edge images and music guide children to enjoy exploring the ideas and tweaks in the designs all around us. An episode on Shells.

programme.title: Somewhere Street
programme.desc: Lyon, about 500 kilometers south of Paris, is France's second-largest city. Its UNESCO-listed historic districts reflect a rich past. It's also celebrated as one of France's culinary capitals.

programme.title: The seasons of Yamato
programme.desc: A series of poetic videos by Koichi Hozan, a videographer living in Nara, in collaboration with a pianist, Mine Kawakami, for which she plays exclusive pieces, makes you experience each seasons.

programme.desc: Abashiri is known for drift ice. Local Glass artisan Gunji Noboru uses recycled fluorescent bulbs to make eco-friendly art inspired by the natural environment while striving to reduce his energy use.

programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Through the Kitchen Window
programme.sub-title: Sakura Shrimp: Japan's Ruby of the Sea
programme.desc: The Sakura shrimp is a rare delicacy caught at just one port in Japan. The locals want to ensure it can be enjoyed for years to come.

programme.title: Core Kyoto mini
programme.sub-title: Maki-e: A sumptuous world of gold and black
programme.desc: The Maki-e lacquering technique uses gold dust sprinkled on a jet-black background. Producing the dazzling effect is a time-consuming process.

programme.title: Easy Japanese for Work
programme.sub-title: Making small talk with a client
programme.desc: Today: making small talk with a client. Raphael Marso, from Germany, works at a company that makes manufacturing equipment. He takes on a roleplay challenge to help improve his Japanese skills.

programme.title: Zero Waste Life
programme.desc: Abashiri is known for drift ice. Local Glass artisan Gunji Noboru uses recycled fluorescent bulbs to make eco-friendly art inspired by the natural environment while striving to reduce his energy use.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: The World Heritage
programme.desc: A selection of acclaimed programs from NHK's domestic channels dubbed into English.

programme.title: The seasons of Yamato
programme.desc: A series of poetic videos by Koichi Hozan, a videographer living in Nara, in collaboration with a pianist, Mine Kawakami, for which she plays exclusive pieces, makes you experience each seasons.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Chatroom Japan
programme.sub-title: I was born in Japan and called gaijin
programme.desc: Imagine being called an outsider in the country where you were born. Chuk Besher knows what that's like. It made him ask questions about identity and diversity.

programme.title: IMPACTS: Climates Change the World
programme.desc: The entire Earth is affected by climate change, occurring with a greater speed and scale than ever before. This program uses scientific data and the latest findings to raise awareness on its impact.

programme.title: DESIGN MUSEUM JAPAN
programme.desc: Sato Taku (Graphic designer) visits Miyazaki. The crowded signs of local bars inspire Sato to reconsider the conventions of modern design, and embrace the appeal of the unknown.

programme.title: The Factory Wonders
programme.desc: Our team visits the National Printing Bureau in Tokyo where Japan's banknotes are made, and gets a rare look at their state-of-the-art printing technology and meet their top-secret banknote artists.

programme.title: Time-lapse Japan
programme.desc: Experience the colors of Amami Oshima in beautiful time-lapse footage. Unique hues include "Amami Blue" ocean waters and the glossy black of silk thread that's painstakingly dyed using wood and mud.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: The Seasons of Yamato
programme.desc: A series of poetic videos by Koichi Hozan, a videographer living in Nara, in collaboration with a pianist, Mine Kawakami, for which she plays exclusive pieces, makes you experience each seasons.

programme.title: BOSAI:Science that Can Save Your Life
programme.desc: The hazard map for Mt. Fuji was revised in 2021, revealing the possibility of greater damage from lava flows. We'll look at what lies behind those assumptions and what countermeasures can be taken.

programme.title: Magical Japanese
programme.desc: The Japanese language is rich in words and expressions influenced by nature, history, and culture. This episode looks at words related to Go. Explore the unique culture behind them.

programme.title: Easy Japanese
programme.sub-title: Which one is the most tasty?
programme.desc: #27 Which one is the most tasty? / To ask which one among 3 or more things is the best or the most, say "dore ga ichiban," then an adjective like "oishii" (delicious), and finally, "desu ka."

programme.title: Culture Crossroads
programme.desc: A supermarket is attracting attention for its handling of both halal food for Muslims and common Japanese foodstuffs. What is the new kind of communication created by such food culture differences?

programme.title: HELLO!NHK WORLD JAPAN
programme.desc: In March, NHK WORLD-JAPAN launched a new online hub for BOSAI-preparing for disaster. It has pages, apps and guides, in multiple languages. We also see programs about June's theme: Mount Fuji.

programme.sub-title: A Taste of Tokyo's Islands: A Special Glass of Liquor
programme.desc: This program visits places across Japan to introduce the charms of their local daily life and festivals fostered by the nation's long history.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE

programme.title: Darwin's Amazing Animals
programme.sub-title: Conservation through Collaboration - White-Bellied Heron, Bhutan
programme.desc: Helping hands from Japan have arrived in Bhutan to aid local efforts in saving the endangered white-bellied heron from extinction. With just 60 or so remaining worldwide, it's a race against time.

programme.title: Time-lapse Journey
programme.desc: Nikko, in Tochigi Prefecture, is home to much natural beauty with its great mountains and lakes. The ornate Nikko Toshogu Shrine is famous. See the widely beloved Nikko through time-lapse photography.

programme.title: Hometown Stories
programme.desc: Ikuno Ward, Osaka, has one of Japan's most diverse communities with people from all over the world. A local NPO provides them with help and tutoring, trying to ensure that everybody can live together.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: While Roger finds an unexpected ally at the Mohawk village, Brianna asks Lord John Grey to help her confront the trauma of her sexual assault. Fergus enlists the Regulators to free Murtagh.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: As Brianna is preparing to deliver her baby at River Run, Jamie and Claire meet resistance when they try to negotiate for Roger's freedom.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: The mood of celebration at Fraser's Ridge is dampened by a mandate from a powerful political figure in North Carolina, and a shocking revelation about a long-standing adversary.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: As Jamie continues to hunt Murtagh with the aid of the zealous Lieutenant Hamilton Knox, he's forced to consider whether or not he's on the right side of history.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: Jamie and Claire help a bondservant rescue his twin brother from their abusive master, but discover that the situation is much more complicated than they had initially thought.

programme.title: Men In Kilts
programme.desc: A road trip with Sam and Graham. In the final instalment, the duo discover how a single hour on the battlefield forever changed a nation and its people.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: Brianna grows closer to Jamie as she weighs a decision about her future, but a shocking revelation soon tests their bond. Roger faces a grueling trek.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: As Jamie and Claire head north to search for Roger, Brianna is sent to be cared for by Jocasta at River Run.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: At Jocasta's wedding, Claire and Jamie tangle with an associate of Stephen Bonnet's.Back home, Roger and Brianna race to fend off a swarm of locusts.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: The Regulator Rebellion reaches a boiling point, forcing Jamie to face his fear and confront the consequence of his divided loyalties. The ensuing conflict takes a harsh toll on the Frasers.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: As the family copes with the aftermath of the Battle of Alamance, Fraser's Ridge welcomes back a familiar face - who seems to be harboring a secret.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: The inhabitants of the Ridge rally around Jamie as he fights for his life. Jamie asks Roger to complete an important task in the event of his death.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: Jamie and Roger implement their plan to eliminate the threat looming over them, but it goes awry. Meanwhile, Brianna is forced to confront her greatest fear and fight for her and her son's lives.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: A revelation about Jemmy forces Brianna and Roger to make a difficult choice. Claire finds that her attempts to make the Ridge safer have dire unintended consequences.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: Claire struggles to survive brutal treatment from her captors. Jamie gathers a group of loyal men to help him rescue his wife. Meanwhile, Roger and Brianna's journey takes a surprising turn.

programme.desc: While Jamie contends with an old prison acquaintance who turns up at Frasers Ridge with his family, Claire re-creates another modern medical treatment.

programme.desc: Roger comes to Henri-Christian's aid after he's bullied by a few Fisherfolk lads who believe the baby is a demon. Fergus is distraught over the child's treatment and worries about his future.

programme.desc: While visiting the Cherokee, Ian encounters a man from his past who dredges up painful memories of his time with the Mohawk. Jamie meets another Indian Agent and fellow Scot, Alexander Cameron, who challenges Jamie's personal convictions.

programme.desc: Claire and Jamie attend a loyalist event in Wilmington honoring Flora MacDonald, and Jamie is forced to toe the political line between loyalist and rebel despite knowing the outcome of the impending war.

programme.desc: A dysentery epidemic spreads on the Ridge, and Claire falls deathly ill. She is almost recovered when the Christies arrive and drop a bombshell on the Frasers. As nefarious rumors spread like wildfire on the Ridge, tragedy strikes.
